text: Prolactin (PRL) response to a second exercise bout during a two-bout exercise protocol is the most sensitive single hormonal marker for diagnosing overtraining syndrome, with 93% sensitivity using a cutoff of less than 200% response.
specific_value: 93% sensitivity, <200% PRL response cutoff for OTS
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS

text: ACTH response to a second exercise bout shows 67% sensitivity for detecting overtraining syndrome and 74% sensitivity for detecting nonfunctional overreaching, using a 200% response cutoff.
specific_value: 67% sensitivity OTS, 74% sensitivity NFO, 200% ACTH cutoff
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS

text: A multivariate discriminant analysis combining hormonal responses (ACTH, prolactin) and psychological markers (fatigue) during a two-bout exercise protocol achieves 98% diagnostic accuracy for distinguishing nonfunctional overreaching from overtraining syndrome.
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS

text: Nonfunctional overreaching and overtraining syndrome can be distinguished by hormonal responses to a second exercise bout: NFO produces greater than 200% ACTH and prolactin responses while OTS produces less than 200% responses, indicating blunted hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis reactivity in OTS.
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS

text: Subjective feeling of fatigue is among the most discriminating psychological variables for differentiating nonfunctional overreaching from overtraining syndrome, alongside hormonal markers.
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS

text: A two-bout exercise test protocol (Training Optimization test) that measures hormonal and psychological responses to consecutive exercise bouts is more diagnostically accurate than single-marker cutoff approaches for overtraining diagnosis.
population: athletes diagnosed with NFO or OTS
